behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch behavior (recursive/descendant event delivery), which depends on OS watcher semantics. MOCK-MISS for this behavior is not just local parsing logic; it is whether real events from nested paths reach the daemon. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is about a file being actually placed in a subdirectory of a watched directory, which requires real FS events; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert exactly one transformed output appears for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The stated failure mode is deduplication logic on same path + time window, which can be exercised at one object/function boundary with deterministic time control. MOCK-MISS scoped to this behavior does not require cross-module wiring unless behavior text demanded queue/worker handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly centers on "deduplication logic" itself, not OS events or module-boundary state propagation; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow due no real OS/network/LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component twice with identical canonical path and controlled timestamps inside the configured window (using injected clock/fake timer), assert first request accepted and second rejected; assert acceptance again after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate FS events. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both apply: in-process fakes may not reproduce kernel/backend watcher duplication patterns, so real failure can be missed unless tested against real filesystem watcher behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires actual operating-system event emission; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI stage exists.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher on a temp directory, perform one write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename or editor-style temp swap), capture processing count, and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is runtime resilience to real directory deletion/recreation while process stays alive, which depends on OS watcher invalidation/rebind behavior. In-process mocks can hide the exact detach/reconnect failure mode. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires actual remove/recreate of watched directory during daemon execution; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete that directory, recreate it at same path while daemon keeps running, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on filesystem-provided creation timestamps and near-simultaneous arrival conditions, both tied to real OS/filesystem timestamp semantics and event timing. Integration with stubs can validate sorter logic but can miss real timestamp resolution/tie behavior. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text anchors order to real creation timestamps at arrival time; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp directory, create several files in rapid succession (including close timestamps), let daemon process them, and assert observed processing order matches recorded creation-time ordering policy (including deterministic tie-break rule if equal).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real concurrent writer/watcher timing and file completeness detection under OS filesystem behavior. Mocked file events frequently hide partial-write races, so MOCK-HIDE applies. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly depends on write-in-progress timing at arrival event; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no LLM/AI stage exists.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, open a file and write it in chunks with delays while keeping handle active; verify daemon does not process intermediate partial content and only processes after writer closes/completion condition is met.
